label: Narrow-shell 2BR / 1 bath, FRONT CARPORT + PUBLIC SPINE — a full-width covered carport sits ahead of the house at the street. Behind it, great_room forms a public spine running back to the kitchen. A short hall (only as deep as the three rooms it serves, not the whole house) branches off the spine to reach master, common bath, and standard.

adjacencies.note: great_room + kitchen form one open LDK, kitchen at the rear — the spine's public half.

hall branches off the spine — open mouth, no door, codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan. This hall only serves the three-room bedroom wing, NOT the whole house depth (unlike 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall).
master's only door — opens onto the short wing hall. Master sits closest to the spine, first in the wing.
master and common bath sit next to each other in the wing — shared wall only, no door.
common bath's door opens onto the wing hall.

common bath sits at the wing's rear edge, directly beside kitchen (the spine's other rear-facing room) — shared plumbing wall, same wet-stack convention as the rest of the catalog. This is the payoff for putting common at the REAR of the wing instead of the front.
standard sits at the rear-most end of the wing, past common bath — shared wall only, no door.
standard's only door — opens onto the wing hall, same as master. Reached without passing through master or common.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_front_carport_spine_bath
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Because the hall here is short (wing-only, ~2-2.5 m of depth instead of the full house), it's capped at 3.0 sqm rather than floored aggressively — the space savings vs. the side-corridor variant is the whole point of this layout, so the solver shouldn't let the hall creep back up to full-length proportions.

setback_elements.note: full-width, front-loaded — narrow lots rarely have side-setback room for a carport, so it sits ahead of the house at the street instead of beside it. Width matches the lot's buildable width rather than a fixed 3.0 m single-bay.

notes: Narrow-shell catalog topology — front-loaded carport variant. Solves two narrow-lot problems at once: (1) no side-setback width for a carport, and (2) a full-depth corridor is expensive on a lot that's already width-starved.
Layout intent (canonical, bedroom wing on the right):
[CARPORT, full width, at street] -> [GREAT (entry)] -> [KITCHEN | (hall -> MASTER -> COMMON -> STANDARD)]

Reuses the same 'reach the bath by walking through the public zone' idiom as squarish's side_split_bath_gr family, but the public zone doing the connecting is the great_room+kitchen spine, not a great_room flanked by two bedroom columns. The bedroom wing branches off that spine through a SHORT hall — only as deep as the three rooms it serves, not the whole house depth. That's the space-saving difference from 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall, which pays the corridor's area cost across the entire house length.

Common bath is deliberately placed at the REAR of the wing, next to kitchen, so it can share a wet-stack wall with kitchen — same plumbing-efficiency convention used everywhere else in the catalog. Standard ends up rear-most; master, being closer to the spine, gets the shorter walk to the entry.

Requires a bit more buildable width than the side-corridor option, since the bedroom wing and the kitchen have to sit side by side for at least part of the house's depth (not fully single-file). Best suited to the wider end of 'narrow' — roughly 7-8 m frontage rather than 6 m.
No fallback declared yet — first draft.
